# Galoy Product Overview

*This file loads into the AI context to ensure accurate product representation across all outputs. Do not editorialize — keep descriptions factual and precise.*

---

## What Galoy Builds

Galoy builds Bitcoin-native banking infrastructure for regulated financial institutions. The core positioning: custody-independent, designed to work alongside existing core banking systems — not replace them.

This distinction matters in every piece of content and outreach. Galoy is not asking banks to rip and replace their core. It's an additive layer.

---

## Products

### Bitcoin-Backed Lending
Banks can offer loans collateralized by Bitcoin without taking custody of the asset. The custody-independent model means the bank can use an existing qualified custodian of their choice; Galoy handles the infrastructure layer connecting the loan origination system to collateral monitoring and margin management.

Key point for bank audiences: this fits within existing credit operations and lending frameworks. The novel element is the collateral type, not the loan structure.

### Bitcoin and Stablecoin Payments
Infrastructure enabling banks to send and receive Bitcoin and stablecoin payments on behalf of customers. Connects to existing payment rails and core banking systems.

Relevant regulatory framing: OCC non-objection letters and state money transmission licensing frameworks govern much of this activity. The infrastructure is designed for compliance, not around it.

### Bitcoin Brokerage
Enables banks to offer Bitcoin purchase and sale to retail and commercial customers. Custody-independent — the bank facilitates the transaction without necessarily holding the asset on its own balance sheet.

Relevant for banks that want to capture fee income from digital asset demand without taking on custody liability.

---

## What Galoy Is Not

- Not a custodian
- Not a crypto exchange
- Not a neobank or challenger bank
- Not a DeFi protocol

This matters in content: never describe Galoy as operating in "the crypto space" or as a "crypto company." Bitcoin banking infrastructure for regulated banks is the accurate frame.

---

## Technical Context (for developer-facing content)

- Open-source roots; much of the core infrastructure is publicly available on GitHub
- Built for integration with existing core banking systems (not a standalone platform)
- Lightning Network support for Bitcoin payments
- Stablecoin support (relevant for cross-border and high-frequency payment use cases)

---

## Tone Guidance by Audience

| Audience | Frame Galoy As |
|----------|---------------|
| Bank CEO | Infrastructure vendor helping the bank offer new products without new liability |
| Chief Risk Officer | Custody-independent model that keeps the bank's balance sheet clean |
| Compliance Officer | Built for regulated environments; designed around OCC, FDIC, state charter frameworks |
| Developer (X/Twitter) | Open-source Bitcoin banking stack |
| Newsletter reader | The picks-and-shovels layer for Bitcoin banking |
